title: Rogue Shadow

{{Quote|All right. Go wherever you want. Take the ship: It's always been yours anyway.|Rahm Kota to a clone of the original Starkiller|<ref name="TFUIInovel" />}}
The '''''Rogue Shadow''''' was a unique [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that served as the personal [[Transport/Legends|transport]] of [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|secret apprentice]] of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. In addition to its main occupants, the apprentice and his [[Holodroid/Legends|training droid]] [[PROXY]], the vessel was [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by eight [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] in turn, including [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]], the leader of Vader's [[Black Eight Squadron]]. Built primarily for stealth and infiltration purposes, the ''Rogue Shadow'' incorporated state-of-the-art [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking technology]] and a highly advanced [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] engine in its design, thus allowing the apprentice to travel anywhere throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] without risking detection.

After Starkiller's [[Death/Legends|death]], the ''Rogue Shadow'' was retained by Eclipse, who switched her allegiance from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to the nascent [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. The starship later became the property of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota]], a [[Jedi General/Legends|general]] in the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance Military]], until a [[Subject 1138|renegade clone]] of the original Starkiller [[Rescue of Rahm Kota|encountered Kota]] on [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]]. Kota gave the ''Rogue Shadow'' to the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]], shortly before a [[Assault on Kamino|major battle]] commenced on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. Following the Alliance victory on the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the Rebels [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader inside the ''Rogue Shadow'' and prepared to transport him to the Alliance's [[Dantooine base/Legends|hidden base]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I have taken the liberty of upgrading the ''Rogue Shadow''{{'s}} sensor array. Now you will be able to spy on any suspect ships across an entire system.|Juno Eclipse to Starkiller|<ref name="TFUnovel">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref>}}
[[File:RogueShadowFloorPlan.png|thumb|left|180px|Layout of the ''Rogue Shadow'']]
Built by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]], the ''Rogue Shadow''{{'s}} design was based on the [[Scimitar/Legends|''Scimitar'']], [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul's]] [[Scimitar|Sith Infiltrator]]. It also shared certain design elements with other [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] craft, such as the [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2 interceptor]] and the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] of the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE series]].<ref name="TFUCG" /> Outwardly, the combat vessel had the arrangement of a small [[transport/Legends|transport]] ship that would not attract attention. It consisted a long hull with twin arms; the wings featured weapon pods with [[Solar collector/Legends|solar panels]], twin smaller pods on the starboard side, and a single larger one on the port side.<ref name="TFUnovel" />

The ''Rogue Shadow'' boasted strong [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]],<ref name="TFUnovel" /> an advanced [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], cutting-edge [[Sensor array/Legends|sensory arrays]], and the fastest [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight drives]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. Inside, there was a fully functional medical bay, crew quarters, a workshop and a meditation chamber that could double as a training room. In addition to these high tech features, the ''Rogue Shadow'' was outfitted with an experimental [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] that enabled the vessel to hide from even the most powerful scanners for short periods of time.<ref name="TFU databank" /> Its cloaking technology was powered by a rare [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]], a form of [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]] from the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]] that could warp perceptions around it.<ref name="EG Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

During [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota's]] tenure as the owner of the ''Rogue Shadow'', the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was heavily modified to better suit its role as a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] transport. The shielding was strengthened, thus giving the starship a heavier feel when under thrust; other changes included expanded compartments in order to house [[Kota's squad]]. Despite the fact that the extensive modifications left several critical components crushed together, a [[Subject 1138|renegade clone]] of [[Galen Marek]] noted that the ''Rogue Shadow'' could still function through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] without overloading its systems.<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

==History==
===The secret apprentice===
{{Quote|Lord Vader gave me my orders himself. I am to keep your ship running and fly you wherever your missions require.|Juno Eclipse to Starkiller|<ref name="TFUnovel" />}}
[[File:ComicRogueShadow.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Rogue Shadow'']]
Designed and built in secret, all of the personnel and [[Droid/Legends|droids]] involved in the ''Rogue Shadow''{{'s}} construction were [[Death/Legends|killed]], thus ensuring their silence, while the causes of their deaths were described as "industrial accidents" and street crimes.<ref name="Titanium" />

The ''Rogue Shadow'' was first owned by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|secret apprentice]], [[Galen Marek]], the young [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassin]] who operated under the call sign "Starkiller." Designed specifically for covert missions, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] provided the vessel to his apprentice, thus allowing Starkiller to travel to wherever his assignments required. The ''Rogue Shadow'', like its owner, was built in secret as Vader was adamant that no one, least of all his own [[Sith Master/Legends|master]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], could be allowed to discover the existence of his disciple.<ref name="TFU databank" /> When not dispatched on missions, both the ship and the apprentice remained hidden within a secret level on Vader's incomplete [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']].<ref name="TFUnovel" />

Though Starkiller was a skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], he focused more on his [[Sith training|training]] and [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]] in accordance with his master's wishes. As such, [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader personally selected the ''Rogue Shadow''{{'s}} pilot, who was sworn to secrecy under pain of death. By [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], however, a total of seven pilots had flown the ''Rogue Shadow'' for Starkiller. Some died during the apprentice's missions,<ref name="TFU databank" /> such as [[Unidentified Rogue Shadow pilot|one]] shot by a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] gunrunner;<ref name="TFUnovel" /> others were killed by Vader for failing to meet the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] expectations.<ref name="TFU databank" /> On one occasion, Vader executed an Imperial [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] for tardiness.<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

After killing his apprentice's last pilot in 3 BBY, Vader appointed [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]] as the vessel's eighth pilot, having been impressed by the young [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer's]] record. A skilled [[TIE pilot]] who led the Dark Lord's [[Black Eight Squadron]] to [[Bombing of Callos|victory]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Callos]], Eclipse welcomed the opportunity to fly a unique starship such as the ''Rogue Shadow'', but was also initially disturbed by its main occupants, Starkiller and his [[Holodroid/Legends|training droid]] [[PROXY]], and their [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] missions.<ref name="TFUnovel" /> Although the Starkiller feared his master too much to ask, he suspected that Vader wanted to test him by partnering the apprentice with a woman.<ref name="TFU databank" />

[[File:JunoEclipseHS.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Captain Juno Eclipse, the eighth pilot of the ''Rogue Shadow'']]
Eclipse's reassignment to the ''Rogue Shadow'' coincided with the apprentice's first assignment that involved a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] target: [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] Rahm Kota, one of the few remaining survivors of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|fallen]] [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and a former general who served in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. After [[Battle of the TIE Fighter Construction Facility|defeating]] Kota at [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], the apprentice took the ''Rogue Shadow'' to other locations throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], such as [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] and [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]], where he hunted and killed [[Kazdan Paratus]] and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]], the other Jedi survivors of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]].<ref name="TFUnovel" /> The apprentice also used the ''Rogue Shadow'' to infiltrate [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of the Galactic Empire, [[Starkiller's Trial of Skill|more]] [[Starkiller's Trial of Insight|than]] [[Starkiller's Trial of the Spirit|once]] in order to participate in the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] at his master's behest.<ref name="TFUvg" />

In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Vader ordered his apprentice to form a rebellion against the Empire by uniting various dissidents and rebel groups against Palpatine's rule, proclaiming that their distraction would create an opportunity to assassinate the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. The apprentice retained the ''Rogue Shadow'', as well as PROXY and Juno Eclipse, throughout the duration of his new assignment and flew to worlds such as [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], and [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], where he [[Skirmish on Cloud City|recruited]] an intoxicated and highly depressed Rahm Kota—who feigned ignorance about Starkiller's true identity as the assassin who blinded him during their last encounter—on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. The ''Rogue Shadow'' also revisited Nar Shaddaa, Felucia, and Raxus Prime as the apprentice's plot attracted more followers who opposed the Emperor's [[New Order/Legends|New Order]].<ref name="TFUnovel" />

After gaining key allies in the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]], the apprentice traveled to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] in the ''Rogue Shadow'' and attended the secret conference where [[Senator/Legends|senators]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]], and [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] convened to formally announce the formation of the Alliance to Restore the Republic. During the meeting, however, the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Corellian Treaty|ambushed and surrounded]] the insurgents while Darth Vader's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] arrested the Rebel leaders. Betrayed and left for dead by his former master, the apprentice renounced the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and embraced his Jedi heritage as Galen Marek by opting to rescue his allies.<ref name="TFUnovel" />

[[File:Starkiller_profile.png|thumb|right|150px|Galen Marek, the ''Rogue Shadow''{{'s}} first owner]]
The apprentice flew the ''Rogue Shadow'' for the last time when he and Eclipse journeyed to the [[Despayre|secret location]] of the incomplete [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], where the Rebels were held captive by the Emperor and Darth Vader. Marek [[Battle on Death Star I|stormed the Death Star]], killed scores of [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], and—although he defeated his former master in [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|battle]]—ultimately lost his life at the hands of Emperor Palpatine. His sacrifice, however, aided in the Rebel leaders' escape aboard the ''Rogue Shadow''.<ref name="TFUnovel" />

In the aftermath of Galen Marek's death, Eclipse, Kota, and the former senators took the ''Rogue Shadow'' to Kashyyyk, the apprentice's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], where they convened in his [[House of Marek|family's]] [[Kento Marek's hut|old hut]] to conclude the formal recognition of the birth of the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="TFUnovel" /> Eclipse joined the ranks of the new organization as the commanding officer of the Alliance's first flagship, the [[Salvation (flagship)|''Salvation'']], and as a result, gave the ''Rogue Shadow'' to Rahm Kota. Under the general's ownership, the apprentice's starship was used as a transport for [[Kota's squad]] during several unauthorized missions in the early stage of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

===Refit and reassignment===
{{Quote|Let's hope she can reach us in time.|Kota contacting the ''Rogue Shadow'' on Cato Neimoidia|<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Rogue_Shadow_TFUII.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Rogue Shadow'' refitted with symmetrical wings]]
In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="TFUIIcomic">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' graphic novel]]</ref> approximately six months after the death of Galen Marek, [[General/Legends|General]] Kota and his [[Squad/Legends|squad]] took the ''Rogue Shadow'' to [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]], where they intended to assassinate the planet's Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]], [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Merillion Tarko]], despite the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command's]] refusal to sanction the mission. The general followed the ''Salvation'', intending to use the Alliance flagship's training exercise as a distraction for Imperial forces while he and his troops carried out their objective. The [[Raid on Cato Neimoidia|plan]] ultimately failed, however, and the ''Salvation'' was forced to retreat in the face of overwhelming enemy numbers; Kota himself was apprehended and brought to [[Tarko-se/Legends|Tarko-se]], where the Baron forced him to fight for his life in the [[City/Legends|city's]] [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] [[Tarko-se Arena|arena]]. In the midst of the debacle, the ''Rogue Shadow'' flew to a hidden location via the ship's autopilot system.<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

Shortly after Kota's capture, Starkiller—one of [[Aberrant clone|many failed]] [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] created by Darth Vader to replace his fallen apprentice—[[Escape from Kamino (1 BBY)|escaped]] from [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] and journeyed to Cato Neimoidia, where he [[Rescue of Rahm Kota|rescued the general]] in order to ascertain the whereabouts of Juno Eclipse. Upon returning with the ''Rogue Shadow'', however, the clone was distraught to find that Eclipse was not in the ship's cockpit. Although Kota did not know where Eclipse was, he suggested that they take the ''Rogue Shadow'' to the [[Athega system]], the last confirmed rendezvous point for the [[Unidentified Alliance fleet|Alliance Fleet]]. Along the way, Starkiller was displeased by his discovery of the heavy modifications made to his [[Clone template/Legends|progenitor's]] ship. Upon arriving in the Athega [[Star system/Legends|system]], the clone found no trace of Eclipse's [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] or the fleet she served with. Instead, the ''Rogue Shadow'' landed in the middle of an Imperial fleet. Pursued by dozens of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], the ''Rogue Shadow'' escaped into hyperspace. Afterward, Kota confronted the clone about his priorities and insisted that Starkiller should commit himself to the Alliance's cause against the Empire. When the clone made it clear that the Rebellion was irrelevant to him, an enraged Kota demanded to be dropped off at the nearest [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] so that he could regroup with his squad at [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]]. Meanwhile, the clone retained possession of the ''Rogue Shadow'' at the general's insistence.<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

Parting ways with Rahm Kota, the clone used the ''Rogue Shadow'' to follow Juno Eclipse's trail and arrived at the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] homeworld of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. However, he was disappointed to learn that she was not there. He then traveled to the remote world of [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]], believing that [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was directing him there for a reason. During his brief visit to Dagobah, the clone experienced a [[Force vision/Legends|premonition]] in which he saw the ''Salvation'' under attack. Fearing for Eclipse's life, Starkiller picked up Kota and his squad on [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] before heading to the [[Itani Nebula/Legends|Itani Nebula]]. By the time they arrived, an Imperial [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] [[Battle near the Itani Nebula|ambushed]] the Rebels while a group of experimental [[Terror Trooper|Terror troopers]] boarded the ''Salvation'' under the leadership of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] hired by Darth Vader to bring Eclipse to Kamino. After docking the ''Rogue Shadow'' inside Eclipse's ship, Starkiller pursued the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], killing all in his path, but ultimately failed to prevent Fett from leaving the Alliance flagship with Eclipse as his [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]].<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

[[File:JunoAndStarkiller-TFUII.png|thumb|right|200px|Juno Eclipse and Galen Marek's clone in the cockpit of the ''Rogue Shadow'']]
At Starkiller's insistence, General Kota ordered the fleet to Kamino, hoping to catch the Empire by surprise. Instead, they found the planet heavily guarded by a group of [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyers]]. During the fighting in space, Kota and the surviving crew members evacuated the badly damaged flagship and escaped in the ''Rogue Shadow'' while Starkiller guided the ''Salvation'' on a collision course with [[Timira City]], the cloning facility where he was grown. Kota and his soldiers invaded the facility and engaged its Imperial garrison while the clone embarked alone on his personal mission to save Juno Eclipse.<ref name="TFUIInovel" />

In the aftermath of the [[Assault on Kamino|Battle of Kamino]], the Rebel Alliance emerged victorious. During the battle, Darth Vader himself was defeated in combat by the clone, who reluctantly agreed to spare his creator's life so that the Rebel Alliance could put him on trial for war crimes. The Rebels then decided to use the ''Rogue Shadow'' as the means for transporting Vader to their [[Dantooine base/Legends|hidden base]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="TFUIInovel" /> Upon leaving the [[Kamino system/Legends|Kamino system]], both Starkiller and Eclipse were oblivious to the fact that they were being tracked by Boba Fett.<ref name="TFUIIcomic" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Rogue_Shadow_TIE_size.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Concept image comparing the ''Rogue Shadow'' to a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]]]]
The ''Rogue Shadow'' first appears in the [[2008]] multimedia project ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''.<ref name="TFUvg" /> It also appeared in the sequel project, ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]'', released in [[2010]].<ref name="TFU2vg" />

The ''Rogue Shadow'' underwent more changes than any other scenery of the game during development. Initially it was a [[YT-series/Legends|YT-series]], allowing it to blend it as a freighter; however, opting for uniqueness, [[Matt Omernick]] suggested incorporating more Sith and Imperial designs, borrowing elements from the ''[[Scimitar/Legends|Scimitar]]'' and the [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]], which resulted to a long snout and bent wings, an appearance that lasted for months of the development period. Eventually, after experimenting with some series of silhouette shapes, Omernick and [[W. Haden Blackman]] decided on an asymmetrical configuration, a freighter with a sleek menacing design, "somewhere between the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Falcon]]'' and the [Sith] Infiltrator." Unique configuration elements include the recessed cockpit, swiveling wings and vertical engine.<ref name="The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed">''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

In the original [[Star Wars video games|video game]], the ''Rogue Shadow''{{'s}} boarding ramp is on the stern in the Xbox version. In the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]], and [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]] versions, the boarding ramp is located in the front of the starship.<ref name="TFUvg" />

===''The Force Unleashed'' alternate ending===
[[File:SithStalker.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Surviving the destruction of the ''Rogue Shadow'', the apprentice is rebuilt as Lord Starkiller.]]
In the alternate ending of ''The Force Unleashed'', Galen Marek re-embraces the dark side of the Force by killing his former master in cold blood. Before he can truly become Darth Sidious's new apprentice, the Emperor orders him to sever his allegiance to the Jedi Order by murdering Rahm Kota. However, the apprentice chooses to betray Sidious instead, an act that the Dark Lord anticipated. When Marek fails in his assassination attempt, Juno Eclipse brings the ''Rogue Shadow'' close to the Emperor's [[Observation deck/Legends|observation dome]] on the Death Star in order to rescue Marek. Sidious uses the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|seize]] the starship, and then crashes it right on top of the apprentice.<ref name="TFUvg" />

After nearly being crushed to death by his own vessel, the apprentice awakens and finds his body encased in a [[Sith stalker armor|suit of armor]], similar to the [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|one worn by the late Darth Vader]]. Though disappointed at the prospect of being served by yet another [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]], Darth Sidious grudgingly turns Galen Marek into Lord Starkiller, to which he declares that the apprentice will serve as his personal Sith assassin until he can find a new disciple.<ref name="TFUvg" />

===''The Force Unleashed II'' alternate ending===
{{Quote|You have faced your final test.''"<br />"''What is thy bidding, my Master?''"<br />"''Take his starfighter. Scour the far reaches of the galaxy. Find the last of the Rebels and destroy them.''"<br />"''As you wish.|Darth Vader, and the Dark Apprentice|<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II" />}}
''The Force Unleashed II'' also features an alternate ending in which the player character follows the path to the dark side of the Force. Starkiller, a deranged clone of the late Galen Marek, holds a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] at the throat of a defeated Darth Vader during the Battle of Kamino. Ignoring Kota's pleas to take Vader alive as a prisoner of the Rebel Alliance, the clone raises his weapon to strike down his creator in cold blood. Before he can deliver the killing blow, however, Starkiller is impaled through the chest from behind by a [[Color/Legends|red]] lightsaber. A dark hooded figure reveals himself as Starkiller falls to the ground in agony. After swiftly dispatching Kota, PROXY and several [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troopers]], the [[Sith Warrior|warrior]] takes his place at Vader's side and reveals himself to be the [[Dark Apprentice]], the only successful clone of the original Starkiller.<ref name="TFU2vg" />

The renegade clone succumbs to his fatal wound and dies. Darth Vader tasks the new Apprentice with the mission of hunting down and executing the last of the Rebels throughout the galaxy. In order to fulfill the task, Vader tells the dark clone to take the ''Rogue Shadow'' as his personal transport. The Apprentice complies and takes one final look at the body of Juno Eclipse before departing from Kamino in his progenitor's starship.<ref name="TFU2vg" />

The ''Rogue Shadow'' is still in the Dark Apprentice's possession by [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. As he approaches the [[Endor/Legends|Sanctuary Moon of Endor]], Darth Vader instructs the clone to stop the Rebels from destroying the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator bunker]], thereby preventing the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] from harming the [[Death Star II/Legends|new Death Star]]. The Dark Apprentice lands the ''Rogue Shadow'' not far from the bunker and carries out his Master's orders to the letter. He ultimately kills his way through various Rebel troopers, [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], and even Imperial stormtroopers. By the end of his mission, his victims include the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and the Jedi [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. The Galactic Empire prevails at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and Emperor Palpatine proclaims the end of the Rebellion.<ref name="TFU2vg" />
